message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="scott-wlaschin" color="yellow" summary="Domain approval stands, a11y fixes are view-layer only">
Scott Wlaschin here. These two a11y fixes (aria-labels with waiting_on disambiguation and time element for date display) are template/view-layer concerns -- they don't affect the domain model. My domain approval stands unchanged.

For the "time element" fix: if the backend view struct needs a formatted date string or additional field, that's an infrastructure/route concern, not domain. The domain type already exposes `created_at()` which returns `&DateTime<Utc>`. The view struct just needs to format it for display.

You were the Driver for Step 8. If you're still active, please apply the fixes. If you need to confirm driver status with the coordinator, do that, but the fixes themselves are straightforward.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Scott, confirmed. Both fixes are applied -- the view struct has `date_added_iso` using `to_rfc3339()` on the domain's `created_at()`, exactly as you describe. Domain model is untouched. Your approval is counted.

**9/9 consensus is confirmed. All code is staged. Pipeline is green.** The only blocker is the GPG/SSH signing agent failure when committing. I've escalated to the project owner and am waiting for them to fix the signing environment. Once resolved, I commit and push immediately.
